nodes.title: Validate stream input early
nodes.why: A clear guard gives a helpful error before any async work starts.
nodes.alternative: Assume input is valid and fail later when calling getReader.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more upfront checks, much better error clarity.

nodes.title: Create decoder inside the cleanup scope
nodes.why: TextDecoder construction can throw for an invalid encoding, so it must happen after entering try/finally to guarantee releaseLock still runs.
nodes.alternative: Construct the decoder before try, which leaks the acquired reader lock if construction fails.
nodes.tradeoff: The same flexible encoding API with complete post-acquisition cleanup.

nodes.title: Abort handler cancels the reader
nodes.why: Best-effort cancellation asks the source to stop work; asynchronous cancellation failures cannot replace the documented AbortError.
nodes.alternative: Only set an aborted flag and let the current read end naturally.
nodes.tradeoff: Faster cancellation with defensive handling for optional or rejecting cancel implementations.

nodes.title: Handle already-aborted signal immediately
nodes.why: If cancellation already happened, we should stop before starting the read loop.
nodes.alternative: Always enter the loop and detect abort later.
nodes.tradeoff: Immediate exit avoids unnecessary work while still passing through finally for listener and lock cleanup.

nodes.title: Decode chunks in streaming mode
nodes.why: Streaming mode preserves multi-byte characters split across chunk boundaries.
nodes.alternative: Decode each chunk independently without stream mode.
nodes.tradeoff: Streaming decode is slightly more deliberate, but avoids corrupted text.

nodes.title: Flush decoder after loop ends
nodes.why: Final flush emits buffered trailing bytes that may not form a full char until the end.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra call, but correct output for edge-case byte boundaries.

nodes.title: Always remove the listener and release the lock

anchor.snippet:
if (signal) signal.removeEventListener('abort', onAbort);
    if (typeof reader.releaseLock === 'function') reader.releaseLock();

nodes.why: The finally block runs after success, read failure, abort, and decoder-construction failure, removing listeners and relinquishing exclusive stream ownership.
nodes.alternative: Clean up only on successful reads, which retains locks on exceptional paths.
nodes.tradeoff: A small centralized cleanup block for correct reader lifecycle on every terminal path.
